project: Influx v1 — PRD 08: LCMA Integration (lithos_retrieve, lithos_edge_upsert, Tasks)
description: Replace the LCMA stubs from PRD 05 in `lithos_client.py` with real wrappers and wire LCMA into the per-profile run flow. Adds a new `src/influx/lcma.py` module containing: (1) a deterministic `lithos_retrieve` query composition helper (FR-LCMA-2: title + up to 3 trimmed non-empty `Tier1Enrichment.contributions` joined by ` | `, internal whitespace collapsed, truncated to 500 chars), (2) an arXiv-ID extraction helper for Tier 3 `builds_on` items, (3) an `after_write(note, run_task_id, profile)` function that calls `lithos_retrieve(agent_id="influx", task_id=run_task_id, tags=[f"profile:{profile}"], limit=5)` after every canonical note write and emits `lithos_edge_upsert(type="related_to", evidence={"kind": "lithos_retrieve", "score": ..., "receipt_id": ...})` for results with `score >= thresholds.lcma_edge_score` (default 0.75), and (4) a Tier 3 `builds_on` resolver that calls `lithos_cache_lookup(query=<prior_title>, source_url=https://arxiv.org/abs/<id>)` (BOTH args required per FR-MCP-3, R-7) and upserts `type="builds_on"` with `evidence={"kind": "tier3_builds_on_extraction"}` only on exact `source_url` match (no fuzzy matching). `service.py` brackets each scheduled / manual `POST /runs` per-profile run with `lithos_task_create(title=f"Influx run {profile} {date}", agent="influx", tags=["influx:run", f"profile:{profile}"])` / `lithos_task_complete(task_id, agent="influx", outcome=...)`. `LCMAError("unknown_tool")` from any LCMA-dependent call aborts the current run, logs a deployment error, and marks readiness degraded (FR-LCMA-6 + FR-RES-3). The webhook digest from PRD 05 gains a populated `related_in_lithos` field (title + score) sourced from high-scoring `lithos_retrieve` results captured during the run (FR-NOT-6). LCMA is a deployment prerequisite (FR-LCMA-1): Influx does not enumerate LCMA capabilities at startup; misconfigured deployments are discovered at first use. `validate-config` is unchanged. Backfill task tagging (`influx:backfill`), multi-profile orchestration, RSS, and OTEL spans for LCMA calls are deferred to PRDs 09 / 10.

userStories.title: Implement deterministic lithos_retrieve query composition helper with golden tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a pure `compose_retrieve_query(title, contributions)` helper in `src/influx/lcma.py` that produces the exact deterministic query string per FR-LCMA-2, so contract and integration tests can assert query content without hitting Lithos.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/influx/lcma.py` is created and provides a dedicated query-composition helper that implements FR-LCMA-2. The exact exported symbol name and signature are left to the implementer provided the helper is callable from contract / integration tests with a `(title, contributions)` input and returns the composed query string
When `contributions` is `None` or absent, the result is the title (with internal whitespace collapsed) truncated to 500 characters

When `contributions` is present, the helper appends the first up to 3 elements in original list order, each `.strip()`-ed, skipping any element that is empty after trimming, joined to the title with the single separator ` | ` (one space, pipe, one space)
Fewer than 3 non-empty contributions is allowed (e.g. 0, 1, 2 non-empty elements all valid)
Internal whitespace runs (multiple spaces, tabs, newlines) in the composed string are collapsed to a single space (AC-08-B)
The composed string is truncated to 500 characters via simple character slice (no partial-word re-wrap) (AC-08-A)

Golden-file unit tests live in `tests/unit/test_lcma_query_composition.py` and cover at least the 5 cases from AC-08-A: (1) title only, (2) title + 1 contribution, (3) title + 3 contributions, (4) title + 5 contributions (only first 3 used), (5) 600-char title truncated to 500
Additional test asserts whitespace collapse (e.g. `"hello\n\nworld"` becomes `"hello world"`) covering AC-08-B

Additional test asserts that elements which are empty after trimming are skipped, per FR-LCMA-2 step 2 (the first up to 3 `contributions` are taken in original list order, each `.strip()`-ed, and any element that is empty after trimming is skipped)

userStories.title: Implement arXiv-ID extraction helper for Tier 3 builds_on items
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an `extract_arxiv_ref(item: str) -> tuple[str, str] | None` helper in `src/influx/lcma.py` that recognises arXiv IDs inside Tier 3 `builds_on` strings and returns `(prior_title, arxiv_id)`, so the builds_on resolver can drive `lithos_cache_lookup` deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/influx/lcma.py` provides an arXiv-ID extraction helper that satisfies AC-08-C / AC-08-D and is callable from the unit tests below. The exact exported symbol name and signature are left to the implementer provided the helper accepts a Tier 3 `builds_on` item and returns either a `(prior_title, arxiv_id)` pair or an empty / `None` result when no arXiv ID is present
Returns no match (e.g. `None`) when no recognisable arXiv ID is present in the input item
Recognises the `arXiv:<id>` form embedded in parentheses or freestanding (e.g. `"FooNet (arXiv:2412.12345)"` and `"arXiv:2412.12345"`)

For `"FooNet (arXiv:2412.12345)"` yields `prior_title="FooNet"` and `arxiv_id="2412.12345"` (AC-08-C). The textual prefix is the substring preceding the arXiv ID, trimmed as needed to match the documented example
For an item containing only an arXiv ID with no prefix text, the helper falls back to the arXiv ID itself as `prior_title` (AC-08-D)

Unit tests live in `tests/unit/test_builds_on_arxiv_id_extraction.py` covering: (1) prefix + parenthesised arXiv ID, (2) bare `arXiv:<id>` with no prefix, (3) item with no arXiv ID returns no match, (4) prefix with trailing whitespace is trimmed

userStories.title: Replace LCMA stubs in lithos_client.py with real MCP wrappers and add envelope contract tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `lithos_client.py` to expose real wrappers for the four LCMA tools previously stubbed by PRD 05 (`lithos_retrieve`, `lithos_edge_upsert`, `lithos_task_create`, `lithos_task_complete`) that issue MCP tool calls and translate envelope errors into typed exceptions, so the rest of the codebase can drive LCMA against a real or fake Lithos. `lithos_cache_lookup` already exists as `LithosClient.cache_lookup()` and is reused as-is by the `builds_on` resolver — it is not (re)introduced as a new LCMA wrapper here.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/influx/lithos_client.py` no longer contains any `NotImplementedError` or `LCMAError("not implemented")` stubs for the four LCMA tools listed above (`lithos_retrieve`, `lithos_edge_upsert`, `lithos_task_create`, `lithos_task_complete`)

Real wrappers exist for the four LCMA tools (`lithos_retrieve`, `lithos_edge_upsert`, `lithos_task_create`, `lithos_task_complete`) with the documented call surface from PRD 08 §5 (e.g. `lithos_retrieve(query, limit, agent_id, task_id, tags)`). Each LCMA tool may be exposed as an async instance method on `LithosClient` (matching the existing `cache_lookup` / `read_note` / `write_note` style) or as a wrapper that accepts a `LithosClient` (or equivalent session/context) plus the tool keyword arguments

Each wrapper forwards the call to the correct underlying MCP tool using the documented arguments from PRD 08 §5 and returns the underlying call result to its caller. The exact return type / shape is left to the implementer; downstream stories (US-004, US-005, US-006) are responsible for consuming whatever fields they need (e.g. `task_id`, per-result `score` / `receipt_id`, cache-lookup `source_url`) from that result

When an LCMA-dependent call fails because the tool is unknown / unsupported on the connected Lithos deployment, the wrapper raises `LCMAError("unknown_tool")` — translating the failure from whichever surface the MCP client exposes it on (e.g. a `CallToolResult` with `isError=True`, or an exception raised by the underlying session) — preserving the contract used by FR-LCMA-6

The existing `LithosClient.cache_lookup(query, source_url)` helper in `src/influx/lithos_client.py` continues to enforce the `lithos_cache_lookup` contract (FR-MCP-3, R-7: BOTH arguments required) and is not regressed by this story

Contract tests in `tests/contract/test_lcma_calls.py` cover: (1) happy-path call for each of the four LCMA tools, (2) at least one `unknown_tool` test for `lithos_retrieve` and one for `lithos_edge_upsert` asserting `LCMAError("unknown_tool")` is raised
`validate-config` (PRD 05) remains unchanged — no startup probe is added (FR-LCMA-1)

userStories.title: Bracket each per-profile run with lithos_task_create / lithos_task_complete in the shared run path
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the shared per-profile run path (`run_profile()` in `src/influx/scheduler.py`, used by both scheduled and manual `POST /runs` flows) to wrap each profile run with `lithos_task_create` at the start and `lithos_task_complete` at the end, so every Influx run is observable as a Lithos task carrying the `influx:run` and `profile:<name>` tags (FR-LCMA-5, AC-M2-10).
acceptanceCriteria: The shared per-profile run path (`run_profile()` or an equivalent wrapper used by both scheduled and manual `POST /runs` flows) calls `lithos_task_create(title=f"Influx run {profile} {date}", agent="influx", tags=["influx:run", f"profile:{profile}"])` such that the full per-profile run is bracketed by the task (FR-LCMA-5). The exact placement of the `lithos_task_create` call within the run is left to the implementer provided the whole run executes inside the task
The returned `task_id` is captured into a `run_task_id` value that is made available to downstream per-write LCMA processing within the same shared run path

`lithos_task_complete(task_id=run_task_id, agent="influx", outcome=...)` is called at the end of the profile run, on both success and ordinary failure paths (i.e. completion is in a `finally`-style block), so the full run is bracketed by the task
Backfill flows are NOT modified in this PRD — they continue to work as in PRD 06 / 07. Only scheduled and manual `POST /runs` flows are touched (PRD 08 §4 internal seam)

Integration test verifies that a profile run produces exactly one `lithos_task_create` and one `lithos_task_complete` in the fake-Lithos recorder. The `lithos_task_create` call carries `tags=["influx:run", f"profile:{profile}"]` (and `agent="influx"`); the `lithos_task_complete` call carries the matching `task_id`, `agent="influx"`, and an `outcome` value (per the `lithos_task_complete(task_id, agent, outcome?)` contract — `tags` are NOT passed to complete)

userStories.title: Implement after_write retrieve + related_to edge wiring in lcma.py and call from post-write hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an `after_write(note, run_task_id, profile)` function in `src/influx/lcma.py` that calls `lithos_retrieve` with the composed query and emits `lithos_edge_upsert(type="related_to", ...)` for high-scoring results, so each canonical note write surfaces related Lithos memory as graph edges (FR-LCMA-2, FR-LCMA-3, AC-M2-5, AC-M2-6).
acceptanceCriteria: A post-write hook function (e.g. `after_write`) is exported from `src/influx/lcma.py`. Its exact signature is left to the implementer provided it carries the `note`, `run_task_id`, `profile`, and access to the current `LithosClient` (or an equivalent shared run context that exposes the LCMA wrappers)

It composes the query via the US-001 helper using the note's title and, when available, the note's Tier 1 `contributions` (FR-LCMA-2). The data source for the Tier 1 contributions is left to the implementer (e.g. structured run data, the canonical note's existing sections, or any equivalent path). When contributions are absent or `None`, the helper is invoked with the title only
It calls `lithos_retrieve(query=<composed>, limit=5, agent_id="influx", task_id=run_task_id, tags=[f"profile:{profile}"])`

For each retrieved result with `score >= thresholds.lcma_edge_score` (default 0.75, sourced from existing config), it calls `lithos_edge_upsert(type="related_to", ...)` whose `evidence` contains at least `{"kind": "lithos_retrieve", "score": <result.score>, "receipt_id": <result.receipt_id>}` (additional evidence fields are permitted, matching AC-M2-6)
Results with `score < thresholds.lcma_edge_score` do NOT produce edges

The high-scoring retrieve results are made available for the webhook digest (US-008) to consume during digest assembly. The exact carrier (e.g. attaching to the note / highlight record, an existing field such as the one on `src/influx/notifications.py`, or any equivalent per-run structure) is left to the implementer

The LCMA post-write hook is invoked after each successful canonical note write in the shared write path (e.g. immediately after `client.write_note()` in the per-profile run flow, including the arXiv source path identified in PRD 08 §6), passing the current `LithosClient` (or an equivalent shared run context) along with `note`, `run_task_id`, and `profile`. The exact placement within the run path is left to the implementer provided every successful canonical note write triggers the hook

Integration test in `tests/integration/test_lcma_edges_end_to_end.py` runs a profile end-to-end with a fake Lithos that returns at least one retrieve result with `score >= 0.75` and asserts the fake-Lithos recorder shows one `lithos_edge_upsert(type="related_to", ...)` with the correct `evidence` payload
Integration test also asserts that a result with `score = 0.5` does NOT produce an edge

userStories.title: Implement Tier 3 builds_on resolver with lithos_cache_lookup and exact source_url match upsert
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a `resolve_builds_on(note, run_task_id, profile)` function in `src/influx/lcma.py` that processes Tier 3 `builds_on` items, attempts arXiv-ID resolution via `lithos_cache_lookup`, and upserts `type="builds_on"` edges only on exact `source_url` match, so Tier 3 extractions feed the Lithos graph without fuzzy matching (FR-LCMA-4, AC-M2-7, AC-M2-8).
acceptanceCriteria: Tier 3 `builds_on` resolution runs after each successful canonical note write — either inside the US-005 post-write hook (e.g. `after_write`) or via a separate helper invoked from the same shared write path — and has access to the `note`, `run_task_id`, `profile`, and the current `LithosClient` (or equivalent shared run context exposing the LCMA wrappers, including `lithos_cache_lookup`). The exact module split, exported symbol, and signature are left to the implementer

The resolver reaches the `lithos_cache_lookup(query, source_url)` contract end-to-end via the existing `LithosClient.cache_lookup()` helper (or, at the implementer's discretion, a wrapper / refinement of it that exposes the result shape the resolver needs), preserving the FR-MCP-3 / R-7 BOTH-args requirement
For each Tier 3 `builds_on` item, it calls the US-002 helper to obtain `(prior_title, arxiv_id)`; items returning `None` are silently skipped
For items with an arXiv ID, it calls `lithos_cache_lookup(query=prior_title, source_url=f"https://arxiv.org/abs/{arxiv_id}")`. BOTH arguments must be passed (FR-MCP-3, R-7)
On exact `source_url` match in the lookup result, it calls `lithos_edge_upsert(type="builds_on", ...)` with `evidence={"kind": "tier3_builds_on_extraction"}`
When the cache lookup returns no match or a different `source_url`, NO edge is upserted (AC-M2-8). No fuzzy title matching is performed
When the item contains only an arXiv ID with no prefix, `prior_title` falls back to the arXiv ID string itself for the lookup (AC-08-D)

Integration tests in `tests/integration/test_lcma_edges_end_to_end.py` extend coverage with: (1) Tier 3 `builds_on=["FooNet (arXiv:2412.12345)"]` with a fake-Lithos cache hit on the arXiv URL → one `lithos_edge_upsert(type="builds_on")` with the expected evidence, (2) same input with a fake-Lithos miss → zero `builds_on` edges

userStories.title: Abort run on LCMAError("unknown_tool") with deployment-error log and degraded readiness
userStories.description: As an operator, I want any LCMA-dependent call that fails with `LCMAError("unknown_tool")` to abort the current run, log a clear deployment error, and mark readiness degraded, so misconfigured Lithos deployments surface immediately at first use rather than silently producing partial graphs (FR-LCMA-6, AC-M2-9, AC-08-E).
acceptanceCriteria: When any LCMA-dependent call introduced by PRD 08 — at minimum `lithos_task_create`, `lithos_retrieve`, `lithos_edge_upsert`, or `lithos_task_complete` — raises `LCMAError("unknown_tool")`, the current per-profile run aborts immediately, a clear deployment-error log is emitted, and readiness is marked degraded (AC-M2-9, FR-LCMA-6). Already-completed side effects earlier in the run (e.g. notes already written via `lithos_write`, edges already upserted, a `lithos_task_create` already issued) are NOT rolled back

A clear deployment / configuration log entry is emitted that identifies the failure context (sufficient for an operator to diagnose the misconfigured LCMA tool and affected run); the exact log level and field set are left to the implementer

Readiness state is marked degraded via the probe/readiness subsystem — the implementation may extend the existing readiness latch (e.g. the repair-sweep latch in `probes.py`) or add a new latch dedicated to LCMA `unknown_tool` failures, as needed

Specifically for the AC-08-E scenario — `lithos_retrieve` raising `LCMAError("unknown_tool")` mid-run — the aborted partial run produces NO `lithos_edge_upsert` calls (because the very first retrieve fails before any edge can be derived), while already-written notes from prior `lithos_write` calls in the same run remain in Lithos and are not rolled back. This is the AC-08-E guarantee and is narrower than the generic abort rule above, which applies to other LCMA `unknown_tool` failure points (e.g. an end-of-run `lithos_task_complete` failure, where edges may already have been upserted earlier in the run)

Tests in `tests/integration/test_lcma_edges_end_to_end.py` cover the mid-run `lithos_retrieve` unknown-tool abort path (AC-08-E): a fake Lithos that returns `unknown_tool` for `lithos_retrieve` after the first note write asserts (1) the run aborts, (2) the prior `lithos_write` call remains visible in the fake-Lithos recorder (i.e. the already-written note remains in Lithos), (3) no `lithos_edge_upsert` calls are recorded for that run, (4) readiness reports degraded

userStories.title: Populate related_in_lithos on webhook digest highlights from captured retrieve results
userStories.description: As a webhook consumer, I want the digest highlights produced by `notifications.py` (PRD 05) to carry a populated `related_in_lithos` field listing high-scoring `lithos_retrieve` matches captured during the run, so users see related Lithos memory alongside each highlighted note (FR-NOT-6, AC-08-F).
acceptanceCriteria: Highlighted digest entries emitted by `src/influx/notifications.py` expose a populated `related_in_lithos` field sourced from the high-scoring `lithos_retrieve` results captured during the run (US-005). Whether the data is attached to the upstream highlight item (e.g. `HighlightItem`) before `build_digest()` runs, or assembled inside `notifications.py` from a per-run collector, is left to the implementer

Each `related_in_lithos` entry is a `{"title": <str>, "score": <float>}` object derived from a `lithos_retrieve` result whose score met `thresholds.lcma_edge_score` (AC-08-F: title + score from high-scoring retrieve results)
When a highlighted note has no high-scoring retrieve results, `related_in_lithos` is an empty list (not absent, not null), preserving the field shape from PRD 05

Existing webhook digest tests are extended with at least one positive case (highlight with a populated `related_in_lithos` list) and one negative case (highlight with an empty `related_in_lithos` list)
No new external dependency is added

definitionOfDone: All AC-M2-5 through AC-M2-10 (master PRD §7.2) are demonstrably exercised by tests
All AC-08-A through AC-08-F (PRD 08 §8 new-for-this-PRD) are demonstrably exercised by tests

`src/influx/lithos_client.py` no longer contains any `NotImplementedError` or `LCMAError("not implemented")` stubs for the four LCMA tools (`lithos_retrieve`, `lithos_edge_upsert`, `lithos_task_create`, `lithos_task_complete`)
`uv run pytest tests/unit/test_lcma_query_composition.py tests/unit/test_builds_on_arxiv_id_extraction.py tests/contract/test_lcma_calls.py tests/integration/test_lcma_edges_end_to_end.py` all pass
Coverage on `src/influx/lcma.py` is ≥ 80% as measured by the project's existing coverage tool
`uv run ruff check src tests` passes with zero violations on changed files
`validate-config` behaviour is unchanged from PRD 05 (no LCMA startup probe added)
Webhook digest highlights carry the `related_in_lithos` field populated end-to-end
